title: Melted Smoky Brazilian Tofu Casserole
description: A plant-based twist on a traditional Brazilian dish, featuring smoky paprika and a gooey, fermented flavor profile, all wrapped up in a papillote for a nostalgic and comforting meal.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and saute the onion and garlic until softened.
Add the smoky paprika, nutritional yeast, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Add the cubed tofu to the skillet and stir to coat with the spice mixture.
Add the canned diced tomatoes to the skillet and stir to combine.
Cut a piece of parchment paper into a large rectangle and place the tofu mixture in the center of the paper.
Fold the parchment paper over the tofu mixture to create a papillote and seal the edges by folding and pressing.
Place the papillote on a baking sheet and bake for 30 minutes.
Remove the papillote from the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve the melted smoky Brazilian tofu casserole hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
